title: The Fabelmans
logline: A young aspiring filmmaker discovers his passion for movies while navigating his family's dissolution and his own coming-of-age in 1950s-60s America.
antagonistOrConflict: Family dissolution, antisemitism, and the tension between artistic passion and family loyalty
themes: The power of cinema and storytelling

actBreaks.description: The family moves to Arizona, establishing Sammy's deeper commitment to filmmaking and the geographic shift that begins the family's transformation.

validation.ruling: Scene #32 shows the family's irreversible move to Arizona, fundamentally changing their world and forcing them into new circumstances - the desert setting and Bennie's presence in the 8mm footage establishes the new stakes and conflicts that will define Act 2.

actBreaks.description: The family moves to California where Sammy faces high school antisemitism and Mitzi's affair becomes undeniable, fracturing the family unit.

validation.ruling: Scene #63 is just a location title card. Scene #62 shows the family driving across the Arizona-California border while Burt tells them about his dream of punching Bennie, causing Mitzi to stare in shock - this moment of Burt's unconscious truth creates the final major shift before the California act begins.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a deeply personal, episodic coming-of-age story that uses geographic moves as natural act breaks. The structure mirrors the protagonist's growth through three distinct phases: childhood discovery (New Jersey), adolescent development (Arizona), and young adult awakening (California). Each act is anchored by a family relocation that serves both plot and character development. The pacing is deliberately contemplative, allowing scenes to breathe and develop character relationships organically rather than rushing toward plot points.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script moves at a measured, observational pace with longer scenes that develop character and family dynamics. Key sequences like the camping trip (scenes 47-57) and the prom film screening (scenes 79-80) are given substantial screen time to fully explore their emotional impact. The pacing accelerates in the final act as Sammy's world fragments and he moves toward independence.

incitingIncident.description: Sammy watches transfixed as a train robbery unfolds on screen, completely absorbed by the cinematic spectacle of the crash sequence.

validation.ruling: Scene #1 is just setup showing a scared boy in line for his first movie. Scene #3 is the true inciting incident where Sammy watches transfixed as the train crash unfolds on screen, completely absorbed by the cinematic spectacle - this shatters his ordinary world and establishes his lifelong obsession with filmmaking that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: Boris sits with Sammy in his bedroom, drinking scotch and looking at storyboards, while Sammy explains he's editing the camping film to cheer up his heartbroken mother.

validation.ruling: Scene #52 shows Boris telling Sammy that family and art will 'tear you in two' while Sammy reveals he doesn't want to make the camping film his father requested but wants to pursue his war picture instead - this is the crucial shift from reactive filmmaking to proactive artistic choice that defines the rest of his journey.

climax.description: Sammy returns home at dawn still in prom clothes, where Mitzi apologizes for hitting him before his swimming test, explaining she was trying to hurt him so he'd be angry enough to fight and pass the test.

validation.ruling: Scene #80 is romantic drama at prom, not the story's peak confrontation. Scene #81 provides the emotional climax where Sammy returns home at dawn and Mitzi apologizes for hitting him, explaining she was trying to make him angry enough to fight and pass his swimming test - this resolves their central relationship conflict and demonstrates both characters' growth.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FOX THEATER, PHILADELPHIA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Six-year-old Sammy Fabelman waits in line with his parents outside a Philadelphia movie theater in 1952, terrified about going into the dark theater for his first movie.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sammy's first exposure to cinema - the catalyst for his lifelong passion for filmmaking

scenes.heading: INT. PROJECTION BOOTH, FOX THEATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The movie projector runs film through its mechanical components, projecting images onto the screen.

scenes.heading: INT. FOX THEATER AUDITORIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammy watches transfixed as a train robbery unfolds on screen, completely absorbed by the cinematic spectacle of the crash sequence.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FABLEMAN HOUSE, HADDON HEIGHTS - HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mitzi rushes to Sammy's room after hearing him scream for her in the night.

scenes.heading: INT. SAMMY’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammy jumps excitedly on his bed, telling Mitzi he knows what he wants for Hanukkah.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FABELMAN HOUSE, HADDON HEIGHTS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Fabelman family celebrates Hanukkah together, lighting candles and opening presents with both grandmothers present.

scenes.heading: INT. FABELMAN HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Mitzi plays piano while the family sings together as Burt films them with his 8mm camera in their living room filled with TV repair equipment.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FABELMAN GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Burt sets up Sammy's new electric train set in the garage, explaining how the electrical circuit works while the grandmothers and Mitzi watch.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FABELMAN HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Fabelman house sits dark among the Christmas-lit neighborhood houses.

scenes.heading: INT. THE GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammy deliberately crashes his toy train into a car with a Noah figure, causing tools to fall and clatter.

scenes.heading: INT. BURT AND MITZI’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Burt and Mitzi are startled awake by the sound of falling tools from the garage.

scenes.heading: INT. SAMMY’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Burt repairs the damaged train coupling while tucking Sammy into bed, explaining the need to treat toys with respect.

scenes.heading: INT. AND BURT’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Burt continues repairing the train in bed while asking Mitzi why Sammy needs to see the trains crash, as she studies her piano score.

scenes.heading: INT. THE GARAGE OF THE FABELMAN HOUSE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Mitzi brings Burt's movie camera to the garage and suggests filming the train crash once so Sammy can watch it repeatedly without breaking his real train.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mitzi introduces the solution that will allow Sammy to explore filmmaking - a pivotal moment connecting his obsession with crashes to cinema

scenes.heading: INT. THE FABELMAN HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Burt arrives home late carrying a TV set while Bennie carries in Hadassah, who grabs his ear and forces him to put her down, as Sammy rushes in asking about the mail.

scenes.heading: INT. SAMMY’S BEDROOM CLOSET, HADDON HEIGHTS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Sammy watches his filmed train crash footage projected onto his palms in his bedroom closet.

scenes.heading: INT. SAMMY’S BEDROOM, HADDON HEIGHTS - SAME
scenes.summary: Mitzi enters Sammy's dark bedroom and hears whirring sounds coming from the closed closet with flickering light underneath.

scenes.heading: INT. SAMMY’S BEDROOM CLOSET, HADDON HEIGHTS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Sammy pulls Mitzi into his closet and shows her his train crash movie projected on the wall, explaining his filmmaking process.

scenes.heading: INT. UPSTAIRS BATHROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Reggie dresses as a dentist while Sammy directs her to put candy corn in Natalie's mouth as he films their mock dental procedure.

scenes.heading: INT. THE LIVING ROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Mitzi sits at the piano to play but is startled by Natalie's blood-curdling scream from upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. UPSTAIRS BATHROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Mitzi bursts into the bathroom to find Natalie spewing fake blood and candy corn teeth while Sammy films the staged scene.

scenes.heading: INT. NATALIE AND REGGIE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammy wraps his sisters in toilet paper and bedsheets to create mummy costumes, soaking the tissue to create wrinkled flesh effects.

scenes.heading: INT. MITZI AND BURT’S BATHROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Mitzi discovers empty toilet paper rolls throughout the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. FABELMAN KITCHEN - SAME
scenes.summary: The mummified sisters lurch toward Sammy's camera in the dramatically backlit kitchen as he films them moving.

scenes.heading: INT. MITZI AND BURT’S BATHROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Mitzi finds multiple empty cardboard toilet paper tubes on the bathroom shelf.

scenes.heading: INT. SAMMY’S BEDROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sammy guides his blindfolded sisters into his darkened room and pulls a rope to make a skeleton lunge out from his closet, causing them to scream.

scenes.heading: INT. FABELMAN HOUSE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Mitzi calls the children downstairs, announcing that Burt has an announcement to make.

scenes.heading: INT. FABELMAN LIVING ROOM - SAME
scenes.summary: Burt tells his children that General Electric wants to hire him because of his work on Bizmac, explaining he'll make more money.
scenes.turningPointNote: The job offer that will uproot the family and begin the geographical displacement that affects family dynamics

scenes.heading: INT. THE FABELMAN HOUSE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Mitzi storms out of the bedroom arguing with Burt about hiring Bennie at General Electric, while she tends to baby Lisa and the children watch from the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTSIDE THE FABELMAN HOUSE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Mitzi emerges with Lisa into the windy, dark weather and gathers the kids into the car to go see a forming funnel cloud.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. THE STREETS OF HADDON HEIGHTS - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Mitzi drives directly toward the tornado through empty streets while her children ask if it's safe, laughing as she accelerates toward the danger.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE ARIZONA DESERT - DAY
scenes.summary: The family drives through the Arizona desert as Sammy films roadkill and his fellow passengers from the rear window of their station wagon.
scenes.turningPointNote: The family's relocation to Arizona - a major geographical and life change

scenes.heading: EXT. THE DESERT OUTSIDE PHOENIX - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sammy and his Boy Scout friends hunt for scorpions in the desert, with Sammy expertly catching one by its tail.

scenes.heading: INT. CAMERA STORE, PHOENIX - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sammy purchases three boxes of Kodachrome film at a camera store, counting out twelve dollars.

scenes.heading: EXT. A STREET IN ORANGE GROVE ESTATES - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sammy and his scout friends ride bikes through suburban streets discussing their photography merit badge project when they spot a group of girls including Janet Benedict.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. A MOVIE THEATER IN PHOENIX - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The boys enter a movie theater to watch "The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance" as Hark teases Sammy about his encounter with Janet Benedict.

scenes.heading: INT. A STAGECOACH - NOON
scenes.summary: Sammy films his sisters and scout friends performing a stagecoach robbery scene with Hark as the masked bandit demanding they stand and deliver.

scenes.heading: EXT. PINNACLE PEAK PATIO RESTAURANT, OUTSIDE SCOTTSDALE -
scenes.summary: Sammy directs the Western scene from the hood of Burt's car while his father helps create dust effects with a parking sign.

scenes.heading: INT. FABELMAN HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Sammy edits his film by examining frames with a magnifying glass, cutting and splicing shots together while Mitzi plays piano.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM CLOSET, FABELMAN HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Sammy projects his Western film in his closet, running scenes back and forth to study the gunfight action, frustrated that it looks fake.

scenes.heading: INT. THE LIVING ROOM, THE FABELMAN HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mitzi performs Beethoven on piano for the family and Bennie, but stops when Bennie whispers about her fingernails clicking on the keys during her important TV performance preparation.

scenes.heading: INT. THE LOCAL ARIZONA JAYCEE MEETING HALL - EVENING
scenes.summary: Sammy's Western film plays for 120 Boy Scouts at their meeting hall while Burt watches intently, impressed by his son's filmmaking skills.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. INSIDE THE FABELMAN’S STATION WAGON/A ROAD IN
scenes.summary: Burt drives with Sammy, comparing movie directing to his management work while asking how Sammy made the guns look like they were really firing.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. ANOTHER STREET IN PHOENIX/THE FABELMANS’ NEW CAR -
scenes.summary: Sixteen-year-old Sammy drives with Burt, discussing his need for an editing machine while Burt nervously watches the road and quizzes him on driving rules.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FABELMANS’ CAR, A HIGHWAY TO THE COCONINO NATIONAL
scenes.summary: The family car pulls a luggage trailer toward the mountains while Lisa threatens to vomit in the backseat.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FABELMANS’ CAR ON A ROAD IN THE COCONINO NATIONAL
scenes.summary: The family bounces violently over ruts in the forest road as Sammy drives slowly while his sisters complain and Mitzi encourages him.

scenes.heading: EXT. A CAMPGROUND IN THE RED ROCK-SECRET MOUNTAIN WILDERNESS -
scenes.summary: At the campground, Burt teaches his daughters about fire-building while Sammy films, as Mitzi and Bennie play together, with Bennie launching Mitzi on a springy tree trunk.

scenes.heading: INT. - A ROOM IN A NURSING HOME IN CINCINATTI - DAY
scenes.summary: Burt sits by a heart monitor as Sammy's dying grandmother Tina lies in a hospital bed with Mitzi holding her hand and whispering to her.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FABELMAN HOUSE, PHOENIX - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mitzi plays melancholy piano with her eyes closed while Burt gives Sammy the movie editing machine he wanted, asking for a favor in return.

scenes.heading: INT. BURT AND MITZI’S BEDROOM - LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mitzi receives a crackling phone call in the middle of the night from her mother Tina, who warns that someone is coming and she mustn't let him in the house.

scenes.heading: INT. THE KITCHEN, FABELMAN HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Fabelman family gathers for dinner when Mitzi's elderly Uncle Boris unexpectedly arrives by taxi, prompting Mitzi to explain he's connected to a warning dream she had about her deceased mother.

scenes.heading: INT. SAMMY’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Boris sits with Sammy in his bedroom, drinking scotch and looking at storyboards, while Sammy explains he's editing the camping film to cheer up his heartbroken mother.
scenes.turningPointNote: Boris's presence introduces the key theme of art vs. family loyalty that will define Sammy's journey

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTSIDE THE FABELMAN HOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: The family sends Boris off in a taxi, with Boris making a mysterious torn-in-two gesture specifically directed at Sammy before departing.

scenes.heading: INT. SAMMY'S BEDROOM/THE LIVING ROOM - 2AM
scenes.summary: At 2AM, Sammy edits the camping footage while Mitzi plays piano downstairs, as he cuts together shots of his mother performing for the camera.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The family watches Sammy's completed camping film in the dark dining room, laughing at Mitzi's antics on screen while Sammy watches unsmiling.

scenes.heading: EXT. AN ABANDONED ADOBE PUEBLO, THE DESERT OUTSIDE PHOENIX -
scenes.summary: Sammy directs his elaborate war film 'Escape to Nowhere' at an abandoned adobe pueblo, orchestrating costumed actors, explosions, and violent battle sequences.

scenes.heading: INT. THE JAYCEE HALL, PHOENIX - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammy projects his war film to an audience of Boy Scouts and parents at the Jaycee Hall, with his family watching as the crowd becomes enthralled by the dramatic battle footage.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE JAYCEE PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: After the screening, Mitzi tries to congratulate Sammy on his filmmaking achievement, but he scowls and shrugs off her touch, walking away and leaving her puzzled and hurt.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FABELMAN HOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: At breakfast, Natalie quizzes Sammy on life-saving techniques while Mitzi angrily makes a huge batch of food, leading to Sammy snapping at her with a cruel joke about calling the undertaker.

scenes.heading: INT. A CAMERA STORE, PHOENIX - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammy tries to sell his Bolex camera at a camera store when Bennie unexpectedly appears, having just purchased something, creating an awkward encounter.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE PARKING LOT OF THE CAMERA STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: In the parking lot, Bennie chases after Sammy and tries to give him a new camera, explaining that Burt deserves this career opportunity while Sammy refuses the gift and walks away.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. THE FABELMAN CAR, A HIGHWAY BRIDGE, ARIZONA-
scenes.summary: The Fabelman family drives across the Arizona-California border while Burt tells them about a dream where he punched Bennie in the nose, causing Mitzi to stare at him in shock.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE RENTAL HOUSE, LOS GATOS - SUNSET
scenes.summary: The Fabelmans arrive at their rental house in Northern California at sunset.

scenes.heading: INT. THE RENTAL HOUSE, LOS GATOS - SUNSET
scenes.summary: The family enters the gloomy rental house with sheet-covered furniture and their piano wrapped in moving blankets, with Mitzi responding despondently when Burt tries to reassure them.

scenes.heading: EXT. A SIDEWALK IN SARATOGA, CA - MORNING
scenes.summary: Sammy, Reggie, and Natalie walk to school with Natalie teasing Sammy about moping, until Reggie demands they leave their family problems behind during school hours.

scenes.heading: INT. THE GYM, GRAND VIEW HIGH SCHOOL, SARATOGA - DAY
scenes.summary: During PE class volleyball, Sammy struggles to keep up with taller, stronger classmates until Chad Thomas deliberately spikes the ball at his face, knocking him down and bloodying his nose.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE RENTAL HOUSE - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sammy approaches the front door of his house and stops when he hears his sisters screaming and glass breaking inside.

scenes.heading: INT. THE RENTAL HOUSE, LOS GATOS - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sammy enters to find Mitzi frantically assembling a cage while a capuchin monkey clings to the chandelier, throwing lightbulbs at the cowering girls, with Mitzi explaining she got the monkey because she needed to laugh.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAND VIEW HIGH SCHOOL - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sammy opens his locker to find a bagel hanging from a string with 'JEW-HOLE' written on it, then cautiously approaches a drinking fountain while watching for threats.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEHIND GRAND VIEW HIGH SCHOOL - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Chad confronts Sammy behind the school about the bagel, joined by Logan and other jocks who make antisemitic jokes, until Logan's girlfriend Claudia tells them to stop and they leave for practice.

scenes.heading: INT. THE RENTAL HOUSE - EARLY EVENING
scenes.summary: Sammy sits bloodied and bruised on the couch while Mitzi demands he tell Burt who attacked him, leading to Sammy exploding that he hates California and blaming his parents for the move.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAND VIEW HIGH SCHOOL LIBRARY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Sammy apologizes to Claudia and Monica for lying about Logan cheating, explaining that Logan made him say he killed Christ, which shocks the girls who didn't know about the antisemitic harassment.

scenes.heading: INT. MONICA’S BEDROOM, LOS GATOS - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: In Monica's bedroom filled with posters of Jesus and male celebrities, Monica explains to Sammy that she finds Jesus sexy, shocking him with her casual attitude toward what he considers blasphemous.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DINING ROOM IN THE RENTAL HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Fabelmans host dinner with Hadassah and Monica, where Mitzi explains her connection to monkeys while Burt feeds Bennie the monkey, and Mitzi and Burt share a moment of deep connection when he understands her feelings.

scenes.heading: EXT. SANTA CRUZ MAIN BEACH - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammy arrives at the beach with Monica's father's professional camera to film the Grand View High Class of '64 beach day, capturing students swimming, playing, and enjoying themselves.

scenes.heading: EXT. 8MM FOOTAGE: THE NEW HOUSE IN SARATOGA - DAY
scenes.summary: In 8mm footage, Sammy films his family's first visit to their new house in Saratoga, showing the excited children exploring while Mitzi stands apart, looking away from the unfinished exterior.

scenes.heading: INT. THE RENTAL HOUSE, LOS GATOS - MORNING
scenes.summary: Burt tells the crying children that Mitzi misses Phoenix and Bennie too much, with Mitzi clarifying it's 'a different kind of missing' when Natalie protests, while Sammy watches from the stairs.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mitzi's confession about missing Bennie 'differently' reveals the affair and destroys the family's foundation

scenes.heading: INT. SAMMY’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammy continues editing his beach film while Reggie questions how he can work after their parents' revelation, leading to a conversation where Reggie suggests their mother felt inadequate compared to their genius father.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE GRAND VIEW HIGH SCHOOL PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammy and Monica arrive at the high school prom in formal wear, with Monica spraying her hair and accidentally blinding Sammy with hairspray before he retrieves something from the back seat.

scenes.heading: INT. THE GYM, GRAND VIEW HIGH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At the prom, Sammy and Monica slow dance while Claudia sings on stage and Logan stares at her with hopeless yearning, as Sammy asks Monica to come with him to LA and confesses his love, but she chooses Texas A&M instead.
scenes.turningPointNote: Monica's rejection of Sammy's love and LA plans forces him to face his artistic ambitions alone

scenes.heading: INT. THE FABELMAN KITCHEN - DAWN
scenes.summary: Sammy returns home at dawn still in prom clothes, where Mitzi apologizes for hitting him before his swimming test, explaining she was trying to hurt him so he'd be angry enough to fight and pass the test.

scenes.heading: EXT. BURT’S APARTMENT BUILDING, BRENTWOOD, CA - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Eighteen-year-old Sammy arrives at Burt's Brentwood apartment building, looking harried and carrying trade papers under his arm.

scenes.heading: INT. BURT’S APARTMENT, BRENTWOOD - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sammy has a panic attack in Burt's apartment, gasping for breath and sticking his head in the kitchen sink, until Burt diagnoses it and makes him tea like he used to do for Mitzi.

scenes.heading: INT. BERNIE FEIN’S OFFICE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Producer Bernie Fein shows Sammy storyboards and designs for his new TV show 'Hogan's Heroes,' a comedy about American POWs and German soldiers, asking for Sammy's opinion on the concept.

scenes.heading: INT. A CORRIDOR AND AN OFFICE, LOS ANGELES - DAY
scenes.summary: Bernie leads Sammy to an office across the hall where secretary Nona tells him to wait for someone who's at lunch, leaving Sammy alone in a waiting room filled with classic movie posters including John Ford films.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sammy's arrival at what appears to be a legendary director's office represents his entry into professional Hollywood

scenes.heading: EXT. PARAMOUNT PICTURES STUDIOS - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sammy emerges from the Paramount Pictures office building onto the empty studio street, stunned and happy, walking away as he gets smaller in the frame until the film ends.

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
